agencyNotes.title: About Us
body: We are two brothers running an end-to-end digital marketing agency out of Mumbai, working across India and the United States.

Malcolm spent his early career at Digitas LBi. Melroy came from RK Swamy BBDO and Ogilvy, where he was a creative director. Between us that is over two decades of agency work — enough to know which parts of the model are worth keeping and which parts exist to bill hours.

A hundred-plus clients later, the thing we believe hardest is that channels should not be sold separately. Most agencies hand you a social retainer, a performance retainer and a web project, run by three teams who meet at a status call. That produces campaigns which contradict the site they point at, and brand work nobody in the media team ever reads.

So we run one system. Paid media, brand, web, social and content share a strategy and a single set of numbers. When we built Beardo from three products on Amazon into a brand Marico eventually acquired, we held the voice, the storefront and the spend at once. That is the only reason it stayed coherent for three years.

We have managed ₹5 crore+ in media spend, mostly for D2C, FMCG and challenger brands. Some of it produced numbers we are happy to put on a wall. Some of it taught us what does not work in a category — less fun to write up, more useful to have.
If you want impressions, there are cheaper agencies. If you want revenue you can trace back to a decision, that is the work we do.

agencyNotes.title: How We Work
body: Four steps, run by one team rather than handed between departments.

1. Diagnose — before anything is proposed, we look at what is actually happening: the numbers, the existing creative, where the funnel leaks. Most engagements start with us telling a client something they suspected but hadn’t confirmed.
2. Plan — one strategy across every channel we touch, with a single shared set of numbers. If a plan only makes sense for social or only makes sense for paid, it isn’t finished yet.
3. Build — creative, media, and the site or storefront it all points at, built together so nothing contradicts anything else launching the same week.
4. Run & report — weekly optimisation, not a monthly deck. You see what changed and why, on a cadence fast enough to actually act on.

Engagements run as a retainer or a fixed project, three months minimum — long enough to see whether a change in creative or targeting actually moved a number, not just a week of noise. We reply inside 24 hours; that’s a habit, not a policy line.

agencyNotes.title: What We Do
body: Eight things, run as one system rather than eight separate retainers:
— Performance Marketing: paid media across Meta, Google and marketplaces, built around cost-to-acquire, not impressions.
— Branding: positioning and identity systems built on what a brand believes, not just how it looks.
— Web Development: fast, conversion-built sites and storefronts — the one piece of marketing you actually own.
— Social Media Marketing: feeds people follow on purpose, tied to a growth number, not a reach number.
— Influencer Marketing: creators briefed to sound like themselves, measured like a performance channel.
— Video Production: brand films and social-first cuts from the same shoot, not separate budgets.
— Content Creation: editorial built to answer what people are already searching for.
— SEO: the channel where this year’s work still pays out next year.
Most clients use several of these at once — that overlap is the actual product, not an upsell.

agencyNotes.title: FAQ's
body: Do you work with startups, or only established brands? — Both, but the model works best once a brand has some signal to build on: a product people already buy, or at least a clear idea of who they’re for. Pre-revenue brand-from-scratch work is a different, longer conversation.
What’s the minimum commitment? — Three months. Enough time to know whether a change actually worked, rather than reacting to one good or bad week.
Which markets do you serve? — India and the United States, day to day. We’ve run US-facing brands entirely from Mumbai more than once.
How fast do you respond? — Inside 24 hours, most of the time same-day.
Do you outsource the work? — No. The people who plan the strategy are the people running the campaigns and writing the briefs.
Can we start with just one channel? — Yes, though we’ll say up front where we think a second channel is being left on the table, if that’s what we see.
What don’t you do? — Anything without a clear owner on the client side who can actually approve decisions. Undefined scope is the single biggest cause of bad agency relationships, on both sides.

agencyNotes.title: The Short Version
body: For anyone who wants the gist without reading the other four notes:
Two brothers, one agency, since 2015. Mumbai, working India and the US.
We do paid media, branding, web, social, influencer, video, content and SEO — as one connected system, not eight separate retainers run by different teams.
100+ clients, ₹5 crore+ in media managed. Beardo is the headline case: three Amazon listings to a Marico acquisition, built end to end.
Engagements start at three months. We reply inside 24 hours. If you want the detail behind any of that, the other notes have it.
